review_items: Purpose: Implement story-scoped changes on dedicated branches using the approved contract, tests, and knowledge base context.

Context: Complete and strictly verify the enterprise Performance lane from the refreshed current project state, including immutable historical receipt validation, without changing the approved implementation or delivery scope.

What those sources say: .sdlc/baseline/BASELINE-ENT-PERFORMANCE-START-001.json: { "id": "BASELINE-ENT-PERFORMANCE-START-001", "schema_version": "0.1.0", "sdlc_version": "0.11.0", "kind": "existing-project", "status": "approved", "summary": "Current Performance implementation context: final comman... | .sdlc/stories/ST-ENT-FOUNDATION/outputs/implementation-evidence.md: # Change delivery evidence ## What was asked? Build the first trustworthy enterprise foundation for the plugin: project behavior must remain stable across plugin upgrades, implementation sources must be discovered cor... | lib/canonical-store.mjs: import crypto from "node:crypto"; import fs from "node:fs"; import path from "node:path"; const NO_FOLLOW_FLAG = fs.constants.O_NOFOLLOW || 0; export class CanonicalStoreError extends Error { constructor(code, message... | scripts/benchmark-foundation.mjs: #!/usr/bin/env node import fs from "node:fs"; import os from "node:os"; import path from "node:path"; import { performance } from "node:perf_hooks"; import { fileURLToPath } from "node:url"; import { discoverBaselineS...

Recorded answers: What remains authoritative?: Canonical files under .sdlc; every cache or index is derived and rebuildable.; May this add dependencies or external services?: No; use Node.js standard library and local loopback behavior only.; What repair is allowed?: Only make strict gate verification follow the immutable profile and receipt that existed when a historical action occurred; never accept tampered hashes or broaden authority.; How is success measured?: The approved enterprise benchmark, full test matrix, and strict story gate must pass.
Validation: branch is story-scoped; acceptance criteria are addressed; blocking tests pass; decision and assumption changes are recorded
Allowed tools: knowledge-base, repo-read, repo-write, test-runner, code-review

assistant_message:
I need your decision before I continue.
Plainly: I am checking that I use the right project context, produce the output in the right format, and stay inside the work you actually want. You do not need to know the workflow terms; answer the questions in normal language.
I will summarize the relevant file contents here. Links and file paths are supporting evidence, not homework for you.
Important: your approval applies only to the item or items shown in this message. If I create a new format, evidence/boundary set, tool choice, work brief, or start confirmation later, I must show what is inside it and ask again unless you already gave a broader scope.
1. Refresh project evidence and boundaries (CAP-PROFILE-CHANGE-OBSERVATORY)
   Why it matters: The evidence behind this internal capability record changed. I must rebuild it before relying on it; refreshing does not broaden permissions.
   What is inside this item:
   - Previous scope: scope project, phase implementation
   - Changed evidence: capability profile CAP-PROFILE-CHANGE-OBSERVATORY source .codex-plugin/plugin.json changed after record creation, capability profile CAP-PROFILE-CHANGE-OBSERVATORY source .sdlc/requirements/REQ-CHANGE-OBSERVATORY-001.json changed after record creation, capability profile CAP-PROFILE-CHANGE-OBSERVATORY source bin/agentic-sdlc.mjs changed after record creation, capability profile CAP-PROFILE-CHANGE-OBSERVATORY source docs/architecture.md changed after record creation, capability profile CAP-PROFILE-CHANGE-OBSERVATORY source package.json changed after record creation, capability profile CAP-PROFILE-CHANGE-OBSERVATORY source test/cli.e2e.mjs changed after record creation
   - Current source files: .codex-plugin/plugin.json, .sdlc/requirements/REQ-CHANGE-OBSERVATORY-001.json, bin/agentic-sdlc.mjs, docs/architecture.md, package.json, test/cli.e2e.mjs
   If you say yes: No new approval is created by this refresh. A material boundary change still requires a decision.
   Scope of your answer: it applies only to Refresh project evidence and boundaries (CAP-PROFILE-CHANGE-OBSERVATORY). It does not approve later format, tool, brief, or start decisions unless I show them or you already gave a broader scope.
   After that: After refresh, the current policy or combined proposal determines whether a fresh approval is needed.
2. Missing work context (contract-ST-ENT-PERFORMANCE-implementation-v4)
   In plain language: I need to know which files, facts, constraints, or decisions should guide the work before I start.
   Why it matters: The brief is incomplete or one of its project, output-format, or tool references changed. I need to refresh it before asking you to approve the current work.
   What is inside this item:
   - Work item: ST-ENT-PERFORMANCE
   - Purpose: Implement story-scoped changes on dedicated branches using the approved contract, tests, and knowledge base context.
   - Context: Complete and strictly verify the enterprise Performance lane from the refreshed current project state, including immutable historical receipt validation, without changing the approved implementation or delivery scope.
   - Context sources: .sdlc/baseline/BASELINE-ENT-PERFORMANCE-START-001.json, .sdlc/stories/ST-ENT-FOUNDATION/outputs/implementation-evidence.md, lib/canonical-store.mjs, scripts/benchmark-foundation.mjs
   - What those sources say: .sdlc/baseline/BASELINE-ENT-PERFORMANCE-START-001.json: { "id": "BASELINE-ENT-PERFORMANCE-START-001", "schema_version": "0.1.0", "sdlc_version": "0.11.0", "kind": "existing-project", "status": "approved", "summary": "Current Performance implementation context: final comman... | .sdlc/stories/ST-ENT-FOUNDATION/outputs/implementation-evidence.md: # Change delivery evidence ## What was asked? Build the first trustworthy enterprise foundation for the plugin: project behavior must remain stable across plugin upgrades, implementation sources must be discovered cor... | lib/canonical-store.mjs: import crypto from "node:crypto"; import fs from "node:fs"; import path from "node:path"; const NO_FOLLOW_FLAG = fs.constants.O_NOFOLLOW || 0; export class CanonicalStoreError extends Error { constructor(code, message... | scripts/benchmark-foundation.mjs: #!/usr/bin/env node import fs from "node:fs"; import os from "node:os"; import path from "node:path"; import { performance } from "node:perf_hooks"; import { fileURLToPath } from "node:url"; import { discoverBaselineS...
   - Recorded answers: What remains authoritative?: Canonical files under .sdlc; every cache or index is derived and rebuildable.; May this add dependencies or external services?: No; use Node.js standard library and local loopback behavior only.; What repair is allowed?: Only make strict gate verification follow the immutable profile and receipt that existed when a historical action occurred; never accept tampered hashes or broaden authority.; How is success measured?: The approved enterprise benchmark, full test matrix, and strict story gate must pass.
   - Expected output: implementation evidence, artifact plus chat summary
   - Validation: branch is story-scoped; acceptance criteria are addressed; blocking tests pass; decision and assumption changes are recorded
   If you say yes: This is not an approval yet. It is a request for missing context.
   Scope of your answer: it applies only to Missing work context (contract-ST-ENT-PERFORMANCE-implementation-v4). It does not approve later format, tool, brief, or start decisions unless I show them or you already gave a broader scope.
   Ask for changes if: Answer with the files or facts I should use, or ask me to rewrite the brief with different context.
   After that: After clarification, the contract can be proposed again for explicit approval.
   What I need from you: approve this item, ask me to change it, or provide missing information.
   Decision needed: Which files, facts, constraints, or decisions should guide this work? Refresh the work brief from the current contents of lib/canonical-store.mjs; do not reuse its old approval.
You can answer in natural language, for example:
- "Use README.md, package.json, and src/ as the trusted context; the proposed assessment format is fine."
- "The sections are fine, but also include deployment risks."
- "Do not start yet; first explain item 2 in simpler terms."

assistant_message_presentation.instruction: Before showing assistant_message to a human, Codex should translate and contextualize it in the active chat language while preserving technical literals exactly. Explain the decision in plain product/work terms first. Do not expose blocking_reasons, status codes, stale/hash wording, or schema keys as the primary message; keep them only as technical detail if needed. If an internal freshness check needs a refresh but the user-approved scope has not changed, say that you are refreshing the internal reference and continuing inside the approved scope. Do not send the user to inspect files manually as the main path: summarize the relevant contents of baseline reports, templates, contracts, and source lists directly in chat, then provide file paths only as supporting evidence. The summary must be substantial enough for approval: explain what artifact was produced, what is inside it, what decision is needed, and what approval does not cover; avoid one-line or ID-only summaries. Do not collapse the message into a bare approval question: show what will be used as context, what output format is being agreed, what work is being authorized, how the result will be delivered, and what the user can answer naturally. By default, a user's yes, ok, or approval applies only to the artifact or decision that was just shown and summarized. If the user explicitly specifies a broader approval level or autonomy scope, carry it only inside that scope and record later formal approvals as delegated automation, not as direct explicit-user approvals.

human_guidance.next_action: Resolve the reported blockers, preserve the evidence, and run the check again before continuing.
